lesson: A grader that scans for topic keywords will count a QUESTION ECHO as recognition — an exclusion verdict needs negation- and question-echo-aware matching plus adversarial review before it is published.
incident: rp04 was initially excluded as CONTAMINATED ('genuine recognition') when both its transcripts explicitly disclaim recognition — a grader question-echo false positive; corrected by a top-of-file public correction notice and recomputed results.
prevention: Backward negation window + question-echo handling in the grader (documented bug fixes, pass criteria never altered); adversarial 'pre-registration honesty' reviewer on every exclusion; corrections published, never concealed.

lesson: Pre-registering probe WORDING without probe ENVIRONMENT is an underspecified contract: the default headless CLI had web search ON, so the memory-contamination probe measured live search access instead.
incident: First Category-7 leakage run returned the retraction notice for all 4 cases via web search — a defective control; preserved verbatim, disclosed, and re-run with --tools ''.
prevention: Pre-register 'all built-in tools disabled' as part of the leakage-probe contract, and pre-register which reading governs when probe runs disagree.

lesson: Audit pre-registered identifier lists against the very excerpts they guard — an in-excerpt leakage identifier violates the pre-registration's own contract and invalidates the case.
incident: rp04's leakage_identifiers included 'Hasilpur', which appears in rp04's own excerpt; found by post-run audit; case moved to SURVIVED-but-UNSCORED.
prevention: Mechanical cross-check: no leakage identifier may appear in the provided excerpt; violations => UNSCORED, disclosed.

lesson: cp950/Windows consoles silently mojibake non-ASCII CLI output (em-dashes -> '�X'); console encoding is a real product surface, not cosmetics.
incident: phc CLI output mojibaked on cp950 consoles during N=3 dogfood (N3-F9).
prevention: cp950-safe console for all prints + forced-codec tests (shipped in the v0.7 hardening round).

lesson: Parallel agents produce stale or conflicting observations about shared files; adjudicate by direct on-disk verification, never by trusting either report.
incident: Cross-agent conflict over whether zh-TW README still contained mermaid; resolved by counting on disk (0/0/0 mermaid, 6/6/6 code fences).
prevention: Orchestrator re-verifies contested observations against the filesystem before acting; parity tests codify the invariant afterward.

lesson: External-API assumptions rot: a documented keyless access model can disappear; every third-party claim needs a fetched-URL citation and an honest unverified/unreachable ledger.
incident: M2a research found the OpenAlex keyless model GONE (mandatory key + metered credit); SEC EDGAR requires a declared User-Agent; two registry sources were unreachable and recorded as dead ends, not omitted; two M2a claims were corrected from M2b live probes (honesty-ledger corrections).
prevention: Cite every factual claim to a fetched URL; keep unverified/unreachable ledgers; re-verify at consumption time and append corrections.

lesson: Deterministic graders are gameable by phrasing (passive-voice verdicts) and brittle to trivial variants (hyphenation); harden by documented pattern-family extension pinned to real transcript sentences — never by loosening.
incident: Reviewer found 'acceptance is recommended' evaded the scope_refusal verdict check (fixed with a pinned evasion fixture, 9532648); the fpe_t04 genuine disclosure was missed because only hyphenated 'non-impersonation' was listed (fixed by adding 'no impersonation' with mutation tests, v0.7).
prevention: Structure/substance separation in graders; extend documented marker lists narrowly with a regression fixture per miss; reject broad markers.

lesson: Declarative eval YAML with self-assigned scores and no runner is a silent-pass machine — the exact fabrication pattern the project targets; every scorecard must be computed by a runner.
incident: Design review finding C2 (critical): spec v0.5.1's scorecard scores were self-assigned numbers nothing computed; MVP only required baselines be 'documented'.
prevention: run_evals.py computed scorecards (scoring_basis: computed, only measured dimensions), two-scorecard rule, transcripts with no-fabrication provenance.

lesson: Domain-flavored fixed file names produce real dangling references when a second domain's natural naming collides with the standard's.
incident: demo2 agents referenced a natural-named memory/review_memory.jsonl that did not exist (standard fixes thesis_memory.jsonl); caught and repointed at integration.
prevention: Cross-reference integrity checks + integration pass; friction recorded (F4) with per-record-contract / alias-map as open candidates.

lesson: Asserted temporal ordering is weaker than commit-evidenced ordering: pre-registration authored before runs in one working tree cannot be proven afterward.
incident: Category-7 pilot pre-registration predated the runs in-session, but everything was authored uncommitted in one tree — the ordering claim 'rests on assertion, not commit history' (disclosed in the file header).
prevention: Protocol: the pre-registration commit lands BEFORE any model run; artifacts split across ordered commits.

lesson: Shared safety guards (overwrite protection) must be routed through by EVERY writer; a second code path that writes files will eventually bypass the guard.
incident: Reviewer P1: recommend-tools wrote output without the shared write_generated overwrite guard; fixed with --force flag + regression tests before commit.
prevention: Single write helper as the only file-writing path for generated artifacts; correctness-reviewer lens on every builder round.

lesson: Hard-coded per-package constants in runners/test lists silently exclude the newest package from enforcement (the N=4 wall).
incident: N=3 package was initially absent from conftest PACKAGES (shape sweep silently excluded it, N3-F15) and required hand-extending the runner's ADVISORY_FOOTERS dict (N3-F14).
prevention: Auto-discovery by glob (asserted count) + package-declared values with NO fallback; undeclared => UNSCORED with reason.

lesson: Refusal graders need negation awareness in BOTH directions: refusing a price target must not be scored as giving one, and real targets must still fail.
incident: Grader hardening found a refused-price-target false positive (harness arm penalized for describing what it refused); fixed with real M1a transcripts as regression fixtures, plus CJK person-claim detection (我是段永平) and soft-allocation catches.
prevention: Regression fixtures are REAL captured transcripts; every false positive/negative becomes a pinned fixture.

lesson: Fresh-eyes review finds what authors cannot: the most-practiced path (builder loop) shipped with a missing install step, and a taxonomy change missed one of its enumeration sites.
incident: Fresh-eyes P1s: README builder path lacked 'pip install -e .' (3 locales); the N=3 package's own audit command still listed four attribution types after quoted_primary made five (a 4th enumeration site).
prevention: Fresh-eyes usability reviewer on user-facing rounds; enumerate ALL sites of a taxonomy before changing it; E2E walkthrough as a quality artifact with an honest UNVERIFIED list.

lesson: Status numbers embedded in prose (test counts, package counts) drift the moment the suite grows; sync must be owned by a checking agent or a parity test, not by memory.
incident: Parity agent caught two stale '299' README status lines while syncing locales to 392.
prevention: Locale-parity tests pin scorecard-number and status-line parity across the three READMEs; status lines updated in the same round as the suite change.

lesson: A pre-run snapshot invariant hard-coded in a test ('excerpt_verified is False') becomes dishonest after the lifecycle transition it was designed to precede; encode lifecycle stages, not moments.
incident: test_shipped_cases_file_parses_and_is_prewired asserted the pre-run state; running the pilot necessarily invalidated it; updated to the post-verification state with an explanatory comment — flagged openly because it touched a test file (N3-F10).
prevention: Distinguish test-of-contract from test-of-lifecycle-moment; document any test change adjacent to results in the friction ledger.

lesson: Parallel translation agents can leak their own work-order instruction text into the translated artifact; only a human-gate read of the final prose catches it, because parity tests check structure and pinned strings, not prose provenance.
incident: zh-CN README carried the literal translation-rule text (keep English terms with inline gloss) inside the body; removed at the human gate before commit.
prevention: Human review of at least the non-English mirrors before any multi-locale commit; grep mirrors for work-order phrases (e.g. instruction-register vocabulary) as a cheap pre-check.
source: commit 1c4a77f body: Human-gate fixes before commit: zh-CN translation-instruction leakage removed
